assumptions: The question is assessed at the consolidated financial statement level.
The intragroup dividend has been declared such that an intragroup receivable/payable has been recognised.

approaches.label: Hedge of a recognised intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item to qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation under IAS 21.

approaches.label: Hedge of a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately recognises foreign currency risk on a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction as a possible hedged item in consolidated financial statements when it will affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 provide a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation.
